LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT CINDERELLA BAKERY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED AS IT DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1113(L). DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO PLAINTIFF YEVGENIA LIPKINA. PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE FACTS THAT YEVGENIA IS A SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO DECEDENT RIMMA LIPKINA. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION ON THE GROUNDS PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CLAIM IS OVERRULED. THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE, AS A MATTER OF LAW, THE SUBSTANCE THAT ALLEGEDLY CAUSED DECEDENT'S DEATH WAS NATURAL TO THE PREPARATION OF THE FOOD. (SEE MEXICALI ROSE V. SUPERIOR COURT (1992) 1 CAL.4TH 617, 630.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-15-2015 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-19-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-21-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-02-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. TONY RICUCCI of Law Offices of Tanya Gomerman, Tel No. (415) 545-8608 appearing for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CINDERELLA BAKERY, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant has not met its initial burden of production. Defendants rely on the Declaration of Dr. Richard Clark in support of its argument that Plaintiffs cannot establish causation. However, the Clark Declaration is inadmissible because it does not "disclose the matter relied on in in forming the opinion expressed." (Kelley v. Trunk (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 519, 524.) Thus, Defendants have not presented evidence establishing "a prima facie showing of the nonexistence of any triable issue of material fact." (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-24-2014 Set For Trial On Feb-23-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Apr-23-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-10-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-31-2013 Continued To Feb-26-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: TANYA GOMERMAN, ESQ., AND JOONG IM, ESQ., 415.545.8608, FOR PLAINTIFF ALEXANDRE BARBASHOV.
KELLY KERR, ESQ., 510.457.9925, FOR DEFENDANT CINDERELLA BAKERY, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT CINDERELLA BAKERY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's request for judicial notice is denied as it does not comply with CRC 3.1113(l). Defendant's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to Plaintiff Yevgenia Lipkina. Plaintiffs are given 10 days leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege facts that Yevgenia is a successor in interest to decedent Rimma Lipkina. Defendant's demurrer to the first and fourth causes of action on the grounds Plaintiffs failed to allege sufficient facts to state a claim is overruled. The Court cannot determine, as a matter of law, the substance that allegedly caused decedent's death was natural to the preparation of the food. (See Mexicali Rose v. Superior Court (1992) 1 Cal.4th 617, 630.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
